events.label: assistant
events.text:
Assessment complete — written to `/app/answer.json`.

Summary: a cotter pin omitted from a castle nut on a suspension/steering fastener is a **fail**, **critical risk**. The cotter pin is the sole positive lock preventing the nut from backing off under vibration; without it, the joint (tie rod end, ball joint, or spindle nut) can loosen and separate in service, causing loss of steering or suspension control. This condition is never within tolerance and must be corrected (verify torque/slot alignment, install correct cotter pin, bend legs per OEM spec) before the assembly clears the quality gate.
